title: Türkiye'de Taşımalı Eğitime İlişkin Yapılan Lisansüstü Tezlerin İncelenmesi
author: GÜLER ve diğ.
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: In this research, master's theses written for bussed education studies carried out in Turkey were examined. The aim of the study is to investigate how the postgraduate theses written on bussed education are distributed according to years, gender of researchers, types, universities, research methods, sample size, research designs, measurement tools and data analysis techniques. It is thought that the research is important in terms of seeing the point reached in the transported education studies over the years and guiding the studies to be done in the future. This research was designed with a qualitative research design and was carried out with a descriptive survey model. The data of the research were collected through document analysis. The data obtained as a result of the document review were analyzed with the content analysis technique. According to the results of the research, by examining the theses related to bussed education, it has been determined that men do more research in this field than women. When the distribution of theses by years is examined, it is seen that most of the studies were conducted in 2010. It has been determined that the theses written on transported education were written in various universities. We see that the majority of the theses written about transported education are primary school students. Quantitative method was preferred as the research method in most of the theses written about bussed education. When the research designs used in the studies were examined, it was seen that the survey design was used more in quantitative research, the case study in qualitative research, and the experimental design in mixed research. In theses written about transported education, mostly scales and questionnaires were used as data collection tools. It is seen that comparative analyzes are done a lot in quantitative data analyzes in theses. Content analysis was preferred more in qualitative data analysis.
